title: Debunking The Biggest Myths About Cheese [Science Explained]

chunks.title: Response to Vegan Doctor's Claims
chunks.summary: The speaker addresses a vegan doctor's claims linking cheese and dairy to lower sperm counts and higher estrogen levels, arguing that these assertions are unfounded and potentially misleading. They emphasize that gaining body weight due to high-fat diets is a more significant factor than the consumption of cheese itself.
chunks.transcript: a bunch of people recently sent me a clip of a vegan doctor saying that cheese led to lower sperm counts eating cheese and milk and dairy and beef fat would make you fat and that would lead to more estrogen and that basically cheese was the worst thing in the world because it was full of estrogen so I had I have to watch this clip and respond um I kind of know what he's going to say here because I've heard these vegan arguments before but I'm going to dive into it and then I'm going to break it down this I'm pretty sure is just complete BS when you

chunks.title: Testosterone and Body Fat
chunks.summary: This segment discusses how increased body fat converts testosterone to estrogen, ultimately lowering testosterone levels. The speaker argues that a high-fat diet can exacerbate this issue, further advising that dairy's actual effects on testosterone are minimal compared to the body’s hormonal production.
chunks.transcript: that's 3/4 fat it's really high in calories and add it to your chicken fat and and beef fat and the fat that people get all over the place you're gaining body weight and guys are struggling with this and mistakenly blaming carbs and so forth but as you gain body weight every fat cell in your body is an estrogen Factory what I mean is your testosterone goes into a fat cell and estrogen comes out you have enzymes called aromatase enzymes that take that testost from your bloodstream and the more body fat you have the more they are reducing your testosterone because they're converting it to estradi

chunks.title: Scientific Evidence Against Vegan Claims
chunks.summary: The speaker evaluates the scientific studies that contradict the claims against dairy, including evidence showing dairy fat associated with leanness and improved health outcomes. They also mention that certain nutrients found in cheese may counteract estrogen effects and support testosterone production.
chunks.transcript: is this impart in your view why there's been a decline in testosterone in men over the last couple of decades without question I can reduce your testosterone level within a matter itself that adds to the estrogen your body is now making in your body fat an animal-based diet is just a recipe for hormone Haywire this is obviously an observational epidemiology study a survey an humans showing that the more Dairy they eat the higher the likelihood they have a lean body phenotype there are also Interventional studies with women who are obese trying to lose weight showing that a high protein High dairy diet led to more lean mass and less body fat there are nutrients in cheese like conjugated linolic acid which actually decrease aroma activity and CLA has also been shown to increase testosterone so the idea that cheese is lowering your testosterone or somehow making you fat is not supported by science CLA lowers aromatase activity lowers estrogen increases testosterone cheese also contains vitamin K2 a hugely valuable nutrient as we know from the Rotterdam study there's a 2020 meta analysis that showed that lowfat diets were actually associated with lower false when you look at the data if you look at meta analyses of dairy consumption whether it's observational trials or Interventional trials randomized control trials the data is

chunks.title: Addressing Inflammation Claims
chunks.summary: Claims that dairy products are inflammatory are debunked through reference to interventional studies showing benefits of dairy, particularly butter, in lowering inflammation. The speaker asserts that scientific evidence supports the inclusion of dairy in a healthy diet.
chunks.transcript: associated with improved Health outcomes more leanness less fat another critical point to note here is that there are Interventional studies with butter showing that it lowers inflammation so claims that dairy foods are inflammatory

chunks.title: Advocacy for Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker promotes the benefits of an animal-based diet, highlighting its positive effects on hormone levels, fertility, and overall health. They express a desire for a respectful debate with the vegan doctor mentioned and share their personal and observational experiences supporting their claims.
chunks.transcript: I'm going to let the comments speak for themselves here I love that he's calling out an animal-based diet but I would say that an animal- based diet of organs either fresh or desiccated meat preferably well- raised fruit honey raw dairy including cheese which you should eat with uh enjoyment that is not a recipe for hormone Haywire that is a recipe for thriving for libido for pregnancy for fertility I've seen this so many times guys the anecdotes are just anecdotes but when there are thousands if not tens of thousands of them we need to to listen up I I love that he brought all this stuff up because every single point was basically something I would disagree with I would love to have a respectful debate with Dr Neil Barnard I'll be waiting for him to come on the podcast uh or we'll do a YouTube video of it in the future but hopefully that helps clear up a lot of

chunks.title: Conclusion and Personal Projects
chunks.summary: Concluding the video, the speaker thanks viewers for their support and mentions their supplements made from organ meats, encouraging better dietary practices. They also introduce their new project involving grass-fed beef sticks, aiming to promote nutrient-rich animal products.
chunks.transcript: there are so many untruths here and I try to include as much science as I can so lastly I just want to say thank you to all of you who find this content helpful thanks for supporting the work that I'm doing thanks for sending me these clips so I can review them I'm so glad there are so many of you out there who are benefiting from this animal based message I think organs are a critical part of your diet if you aren't getting enough liver and hard in your diet check out heart and soil supplements where we make the finest grass-fed desicated organs on the planet and very exciting in case you didn't hear I have a new project that has recently launched it is called lineage and we are making grass-fed regeneratively raised beef sticks with heart and liver check us out at lineage provisions.com love you guys see you in
